name: Kinematic multi-agent driving env (vectorized JAX)
role: The TRAINING substrate. A fast, GPU-vectorized multi-agent driving environment on the real SF road graph where the coordination policy learns. Cars move via a kinematic-bicycle model; the env exposes step(setpoints)->(obs,reward).
decision: BUILT in smoothride/env/kinematic.py (JAX, vmap-able). Kinematic-bicycle motion with accel/steer limits; setpoint-style actions for clean transfer to the Isaac low-level controller. v2 implements: per-edge speed limits (highways), multi-lane + lane-change action, lead-vehicle gap (stop-and-go), intersection right-of-way/yielding, unruly pedestrians, continuous respawn (throughput metric), and APF spacing. REMAINING ROADMAP: spatial-hash neighbors for 1000+ cars (currently O(N^2), fine to a few hundred); per-edge grade coupled with terrain-dem; OSM-tied pedestrian crossings.
inputs: osmnx road graph (lanes/waypoints/oneway) as the drivable network
per-agent HIGH-LEVEL actions = setpoints (target waypoint / velocity / heading)
outputs: per-agent observations (ego state + nearby cars + local route)
collision/near-miss signals (geometric overlap, no physics engine needed)
why_kinematic: The traffic-coordination claim ('zero crashes, no gridlock, no lights') lives at the behavioral level, not tire-slip level.
Kinematic bicycle is the correct vehicle model for traffic-level behavior and is valid for gentle/normal driving.
Fully vectorizable in JAX -> orders of magnitude faster than a rigid-body sim -> real RL in hackathon time.
Eliminates the OSM->3D-collision-mesh dependency for TRAINING (graph-only).
interface_to_physics: Action space = setpoints the frozen low-level controller (Isaac) can also consume.
Match accel/steering-rate limits to the WheeledLab car so the kinematic<->physics tracking gap stays small.
Keep learned driving gentle (the gap grows with aggressive maneuvers).
alternatives: MetaDrive / SMARTS (purpose-built multi-agent driving RL) — consider if you'd rather not hand-build; cost = their API + real-map import friction
risks: kinematic<->physics interface gap if driving gets aggressive -> match dynamics limits, keep gentle
collision proxy must be calibrated (car footprint) to be a meaningful no-crash signal
hand-rolled env = you own correctness; budget testing
